cards.altText: Chrysler Neon ECC engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Ignition coil failure - causes intermittent misfires and rough idling, often requiring coil pack replacement.
Valve cover gasket leaks - oil seeps into spark plug tubes, fouling plugs and worsening misfires.

cards.altText: Chrysler Neon ECB engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Oil consumption - worn piston rings lead to increased oil burn, particularly on higher-mileage examples.
Startup rattling - timing belt tensioner wear causes a brief rattle on cold starts.

cards.altText: Chrysler Neon 1.6L engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Camshaft position sensor failure - leads to intermittent stalling or no-start conditions on aging units.

commonProblems.h2: Chrysler Neon Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Chrysler Neon’s reliability is generally robust, but age-related issues like ignition coil failures, valve cover gasket oil leaks, and timing belt tensioner wear are common. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address these original failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Chrysler Neon Ignition Coil Failure - Why Engines Misfire Under Load
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Neon SE, LE, R/T (1994-2005, ECC & ECB)
problems.rootCause: The coil pack degrades over time due to heat cycling, leading to weak spark delivery and intermittent misfires, particularly during acceleration.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removing the old coil pack and installing a new OEM-specification unit.
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early, but does not address underlying spark plug or wiring degradation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Installing a reconditioned engine with a brand-new ignition system, resolving all misfire sources.
repairOptions.longevity: The definitive fix if misfires have caused secondary damage or if the engine has high overall mileage.

problems.recommendation: If the misfire is isolated to the coil pack, a simple replacement is the most cost-effective route. However, if the engine has high mileage and multiple electrical faults, a rebuilt engine provides long-term peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt ECC/ECB engine prices for your Chrysler Neon

problems.group: Chrysler Neon Valve Cover Gasket Leak
problems.h4: Chrysler Neon Valve Cover Gasket Leak - Why Oil Fouls Spark Plugs
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Neon LX, SE, LE, R/T (1994-2005, ECC & ECB)
problems.rootCause: The rubber gasket hardens and shrinks over time, allowing oil to seep into the spark plug tubes, fouling the plugs and degrading ignition performance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removing the valve cover, cleaning the surface, and fitting a new gasket and spark plug tube seals.
repairOptions.longevity: A highly effective, low-cost repair if the engine is otherwise in good mechanical health.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: A full reconditioned engine naturally includes all-new seals and gaskets, eliminating the leak permanently.
repairOptions.longevity: Best suited for high-mileage engines where the gasket leak is just one of several age-related issues.

problems.recommendation: For most Neon owners, replacing the valve cover gasket and tube seals is a straightforward and inexpensive fix. Only consider engine replacement if the oil starvation has caused broader internal damage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Chrysler Neon engine replacement from UK specialists

problems.h4: Chrysler Neon Timing Belt Snap - The Risk of Interference Damage
problems.affectedModels: All Chrysler Neon variants (1994-2005, ECC, ECB, EDZ)
problems.rootCause: The Neon is an interference engine. If the timing belt snaps or jumps due to age or a failed tensioner, the pistons will strike the open valves, causing catastrophic internal damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing the timing belt, tensioner, and idler pulleys before failure occurs.
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance. Highly recommended every 60k miles or 5 years.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Installing a reconditioned engine with a new timing belt kit already fitted and verified.
repairOptions.longevity: The only viable solution if the belt has already snapped and bent the valves.

problems.recommendation: Never delay a timing belt change on a Chrysler Neon. If the belt has already failed, do not attempt to crank the engine. A rebuilt engine is often more reliable and cost-effective than attempting to salvage a bent-valve cylinder head.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Neon engine prices to avoid catastrophic failure

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Chrysler Neon
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're battling misfires, oil leaks, or the constant worry of a timing belt failure, EngineMarket makes it simple to find a lasting solution. Our network of trusted UK Chrysler Neon specialists provides rebuilt and reconditioned engines with upgraded components to fix original design flaws, all backed by a 12-month warranty and available with nationwide supply & fit. Compare prices today and get back on the road without paying the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Chrysler Neon Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Chrysler Neon and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Chrysler Neon Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine configurations for the Chrysler Neon from its 1994 launch to its 2005 end-of-production, so you can quickly identify your engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: First Generation: The Original PL Chassis
years.description: The Chrysler Neon launched with a fresh, aerodynamic design and a choice of simple, robust 1.6L and 2.0L petrol engines, quickly gaining a reputation as a fun, affordable compact car.
keyChanges: Introduction of the Chrysler Neon to replace the older Chrysler/Plymouth models
Launch of the 1.6L 16V and 2.0L SOHC/DOHC engine options
Availability of LX, SE, LE, and the sporty R/T trim levels
knownFor: The 2.0 DOHC providing surprising performance for its class
Early models now considered affordable modern classics
Timing belt maintenance is critical due to interference engine design
years.cta: Get quotes for 1994-1999 Chrysler Neon replacement engines
years.ticker: Chrysler Neon 1994 engine replacement • Chrysler Neon 2.0 timing belt fix • Neon valve cover leak repair

years.description: The second-generation Neon received a mild facelift, improved interior materials, and refined engine management systems, though the core powertrain architecture remained unchanged.
keyChanges: Subtle exterior and interior design updates for a more modern look
Introduction of the CS (Cruising Sedan) trim in some markets
Updated ECU mapping for better emissions and fuel economy
mainEngines: EDZ - 2.4L petrol (select CS and export models)
knownFor: Continued reliability of the 2.0L SOHC engine
Slightly improved fuel economy over the first generation
Final years of production before the model's discontinuation in 2005
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000-2005 Chrysler Neon replacement engines

faq.h2: Chrysler Neon Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Chrysler Neon engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A Chrysler Neon engine replacement typically costs between £1,200 and £2,500 for a rebuilt or reconditioned unit, supply only. Adding supply and fit will increase this by around £400-£800.
keyPoints: 1.6L and 2.0L SOHC engines are the most affordable options.
2.0L DOHC and 2.4L variants may cost slightly more due to demand.
Prices vary based on the specific engine code and supplier.
Supply and fit packages offer a single, consolidated warranty.
items.cta: Compare Chrysler Neon engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What is the most reliable engine for a Chrysler Neon?
items.answer: The most reliable replacement is a professionally rebuilt 2.0L SOHC (ECB) engine, as it features a simpler valvetrain and fewer high-stress components than the DOHC variant.
keyPoints: A rebuilt engine corrects known failure points with new parts.
The ECB engine is less prone to high-RPM wear than the ECC.
Avoid cheap used engines for high-mileage vehicles with known issues.
Always choose a unit with a minimum 12-month warranty.
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Chrysler Neon engine with 12-month warranty

items.question: Can I fit a 2.0L engine into a 1.6L Chrysler Neon?
items.answer: While physically possible, swapping a 2.0L engine into a 1.6L Neon requires significant modifications to the ECU, wiring harness, and engine mounts, making it impractical for most owners.
keyPoints: Official cross-compatibility is not supported by the manufacturer.
ECU remapping and custom wiring are essential for the swap to function.
Insurance complications may arise from unapproved engine modifications.
We strongly recommend direct, OEM-code replacements for reliability.
items.cta: Enter your reg to find the correct engine for your Chrysler Neon year

items.question: What are the common engine codes for the Chrysler Neon?
items.answer: Your engine code can be found on your V5C logbook or stamped on the engine block. The most common codes are ECC (2.0L DOHC), ECB (2.0L SOHC), and EDZ (2.4L).
keyPoints: The 8th VIN digit can also help identify your engine family.
Your V5C logbook will list the engine code under 'engine number'.
Providing the exact code ensures you get the correct replacement.
Our system instantly matches your reg to the precise engine code.
items.cta: Enter your reg to instantly find your Chrysler Neon engine code

items.question: Do you offer a warranty on reconditioned Chrysler Neon engines?
items.answer: Yes, all reconditioned and rebuilt Chrysler Neon engines supplied through our network come with a minimum 12-month, unlimited-mileage warranty as standard.
keyPoints: The warranty covers all internal mechanical parts and gaskets.
Labour for warranty claims is often included via our supply & fit network.
This provides crucial peace of mind compared to unverified used engines.
Always confirm the exact terms with your chosen supplier before purchasing.
items.cta: Get quotes for Chrysler Neon engine replacement from UK specialists

items.question: How long does a Chrysler Neon engine supply and fit take?
items.answer: A professional supply and fit of a Chrysler Neon engine typically takes 1 to 2 days once the engine has arrived at the garage, with total turnaround times of 3 to 5 working days.
keyPoints: We coordinate next-day delivery of the engine to your local garage.
The Neon's engine bay is relatively accessible, aiding faster installation.
You receive a single invoice covering both the engine and the labour.
This eliminates the hassle of sourcing a separate fitting service.
items.cta: Book your Chrysler Neon engine supply and fit today

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Chrysler Neon?
items.answer: Yes, if the rest of the car (bodywork, transmission, interior) is in good condition, a £1,500-£2,500 engine replacement is far more economical than scrapping the car.
keyPoints: The Chrysler Neon has low residual value, making dealer quotes unviable.
A specialist-supplied rebuilt engine restores reliability for a fraction of the cost.
It extends the life of a vehicle you are already familiar with and enjoy.
It is significantly cheaper than financing a newer, unknown used car.
items.cta: Get a free quote to see if engine replacement is right for you

items.question: What causes a Chrysler Neon engine to misfire?
items.answer: Misfires are most commonly caused by a failing ignition coil pack or oil-fouled spark plugs resulting from a leaking valve cover gasket, both of which are easily resolved.
keyPoints: The coil pack degrades over time, leading to weak spark delivery.
Oil seeping into the spark plug tubes ruins the plugs and causes misfires.
A simple coil or gasket replacement often fixes the issue entirely.
If the misfire persists, internal compression loss may require engine replacement.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt ECC/ECB engine prices for your Chrysler Neon

items.question: Can you source a used Chrysler Neon engine with low mileage?
items.answer: Yes, we can source verified low-mileage used Chrysler Neon engines from our network, all of which undergo basic compression and leak-down testing before dispatch.
keyPoints: We connect you with over 100 UK engine specialists and breakers.
Mileage verification and history checks are prioritised for all used units.
You can compare price, mileage, and warranty side-by-side online.
Delivery to your chosen garage is arranged seamlessly by our team.
items.cta: Check used Chrysler Neon engine availability nationwide

items.question: Do I need to replace the timing belt with a new engine?
items.answer: Yes, we strongly recommend concurrently replacing the timing belt, water pump, and tensioners when installing a new engine, as it is a critical preventative measure.
keyPoints: The Neon is an interference engine; belt failure causes catastrophic damage.
The labour cost to access the belt is already covered during the engine swap.
Replacing these components prevents future, costly breakdowns.
Most specialist fitters will include a new timing kit as standard practice.
items.cta: Get quotes for Chrysler Neon engine replacement with new timing kit
